title: The Four Barons of Hell
wikitext:
[]: The Four Barons of Hell             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Dec 10, 2005 11:50 am While on Otaku Generation , I was reminded of the now (hopefully) firmly debunked notion of the Evangelions being based on the four "barons of hell" . For those of you not familiar with this, let me break it down for you. The idea is that there are four demons/devils in some Christian biblical/apocryphal text that are known as the "barons of hell". One has a large singular eye, one has a single horn, one is blood red with four eyes and two mouths, and the last is completely black with exceedingly long arms. These barons are sometimes referred to as being demons, devils, or originating from the Babylonian gods. This is even brought up as being true and legitimate on the Manga Entertainment audio commentary for the Eva movies. Well, make no mistake about it, this is one bogus fan theory, seemingly hatched to confuse and possibly amuse its creators. Well, I came back across this fairly extensive peice that goes through the various explanations and sources on this, and thoroughly debunks this. An interesting read to say the least.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Sat Dec 10, 2005 2:52 pm i believed this theory untill today when i read said article. how gullable we are.

[]:             Quote Post by Punio4 » Sat Dec 10, 2005 4:35 pm heh... heard something about this before... but didn't buy it 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Ion » Sun Dec 11, 2005 6:44 pm Well I'm not sure but here:http://web.archive.org/web/20030212170448/http://masterwork.animemedia.com/Evangelion/anno.html On the unique appearance of the Evangelion Units... ANNO: There is a monster in Japan called the oni, which has two horns sticking out of its head, and the overall image of the EVA is based on that. I wanted also to have an image that beneath the image of that robot monster is a human. It's not really a robot, but a giant human, so it's different from other robot mecha such as those in Gundam. Do you know anything about Oni ?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 11, 2005 7:25 pm Oni - roughly equivalent to an ogre.

[]: Re: The Four Barons of Hell             Quote Post by chiefen » Sun Dec 11, 2005 9:23 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: While on Otaku Generation , I was reminded of the now (hopefully) firmly debunked notion of the Evangelions being based on the four "barons of hell" . For those of you not familiar with this, let me break it down for you. The idea is that there are four demons/devils in some Christian biblical/apocryphal text that are known as the "barons of hell". One has a large singular eye, one has a single horn, one is blood red with four eyes and two mouths, and the last is completely black with exceedingly long arms. These barons are sometimes referred to as being demons, devils, or originating from the Babylonian gods. This is even brought up as being true and legitimate on the Manga Entertainment audio commentary for the Eva movies. Well, make no mistake about it, this is one bogus fan theory, seemingly hatched to confuse and possibly amuse its creators. Well, I came back across this fairly extensive peice that goes through the various explanations and sources on this, and thoroughly debunks this. An interesting read to say the least.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Sun Dec 11, 2005 10:00 pm Ion wrote: Do you know anything about Oni ?

[]: Re: The Four Barons of Hell             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Dec 11, 2005 11:01 pm chiefen wrote: What are the names of these four barons?

[]: Re: The Four Barons of Hell             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 12, 2005 2:18 am The Eva Monkey wrote: They don't have names. They're not real .

[]: Re: The Four Barons of Hell             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 12, 2005 7:09 am Mr. Tines wrote: A big clue is that "baron" is the lowest grade of nobility - if you go to one of the traditional syntheses such as the Lesser Key of Solomon the King , you see that there's a whole panoply of "higher" ranks occupied in the Infernal Hierarchy.

[]:             Quote Post by acemagician » Tue Dec 13, 2005 4:17 pm Now we all know the truth. And if I might say, Anno's version of the origins of the EVAs is much more itresting from the "barons of hell" version.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Thu Dec 15, 2005 11:59 pm Not to mention that its more realistic to pick the oni idea then some long unknow (and just now ive seen its not REAL) myth. If i ever met the person who created this 4 barron thingy, im gana kick the square in the nuts!

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Fri Dec 16, 2005 11:09 am This show goes pretty far into detail...
